frameworks.name: successor representation
frameworks.one_line: temporally abstracted predictive map of future state occupancies guiding choice evaluation

frameworks.name: model-based mental simulation
frameworks.one_line: step-by-step trajectory rollout as an alternative to cached predictive summaries

frameworks.name: predictive neural representations
frameworks.one_line: sensory-cortical decoding of upcoming states linked to SR-specific expectancy errors

core_question: Do predictive neural representations during choice evaluation support choices in a manner consistent with the successor representation?
key_assumptions: the brain caches multi-step action consequences rather than simulating each step
SR predicts specific erroneous multi-step expectancies in suitable tasks
sensory cortex carries decodable representations of predicted upcoming states
manipulations: multi-step task structure designed so SR implies specific expectancy errors
decoding of state predictions from sensory cortex
key_findings: behavioral slips of action consistent with SR-predicted errors are observed
predictive sensory-cortical representations of upcoming states accompany those errors
decoded neural predictions match SR-implied erroneous multi-step expectancies
interpretations: predictive neural representations support choice evaluation in an SR-consistent way
temporal abstraction via cached successors underlies multi-step value computation
